title: Premodern, Modern, Postmodernism And Buddhism
justification: This talk directly addresses the chapter theme with a comprehensive framework examining pre-modern, modern, and postmodern societies through a Buddhist lens. It offers rich structural organization (three distinct worldviews with corresponding teacher-student relationships, character types, and scriptural examples), engaging anecdotes (the anthill teaching, Tuchaputila, the postmodern picnic), and substantive analysis of how Buddhism can function within each paradigm. The talk concludes by showing how the Buddha's teaching transcends all three approaches, making it ideal as a spine for grafting complementary material.
summary: Bhante Bodhidhamma systematically examines pre-modern (faith-based revelation), modern (rational inquiry), and postmodern (subjective interpretation) worldviews, analyzing how each shapes the teacher-student relationship and spiritual practice. He demonstrates how the Buddha's teaching uniquely incorporates elements of all three while pointing beyond them to liberation.

relevance.note: Directly examines postmodernism and its relationship to Buddhism
